project_section: Transformer experiments optimized and selected using average precision
purpose: This folder summarizes the transformer experiment in a handoff-friendly format. The shared multilabel transformer now uses cross-validation and multiple epochs, with checkpoint selection based on validation mean average precision. The binary transformers keep the simpler design but are also selected using validation average precision.

how_to_read_the_main_metric.average_precision: For binary models, this is the primary metric. Higher is better.
how_to_read_the_main_metric.mean_average_precision: For the multilabel model, this is the mean of label-level average precision values and is the primary metric.
how_to_read_the_main_metric.why_not_f1: Average precision is threshold-free and better suited for imbalanced labels. F1 at 0.5 is still saved for diagnostic use.

models.plain_english_summary: One shared transformer predicts all six labels together and is chosen using validation mean average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only toxic and is chosen using validation average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only severe_toxic and is chosen using validation average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only obscene and is chosen using validation average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only threat and is chosen using validation average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only insult and is chosen using validation average precision.
One specialized transformer predicts only identity_hate and is chosen using validation average precision.

recommended_interpretation: Use the shared multilabel CV model as the main transformer benchmark.
Use the binary models as specialization studies for individual labels.
Discuss both predictive quality and systems cost; six binary models are more expensive to train and maintain than one shared model.

threshold_tuning_postprocessing.validation_split_requirement: thresholds were selected using saved validation probabilities only
threshold_tuning_postprocessing.average_precision_note: Average precision remains threshold-free and should be unchanged aside from floating point roundoff.
